openmeter/streaming/clickhouse.purpose: Concrete streaming.Connector implementation against ClickHouse's single shared events table (ORDER BY namespace, type, subject, toStartOfHour(time)). Every query type is a distinct query-struct with a toSQL() method, keeping business logic out of the Connector and enabling SQL-assertion unit tests without a live ClickHouse.

patterns.name: Query-struct with toSQL()
patterns.description: Each query type (queryMeter, queryEventsTable, InsertEventsQuery, listSubjectsQuery) is a plain struct with a toSQL()/ToSQL() returning (string, []interface{}). Connector methods are thin delegators that build the struct and call toSQL(); all logic lives in the struct.
patterns.example: query := queryMeter{Database: c.config.Database, EventsTableName: c.config.EventsTableName, Namespace: namespace, Meter: meter}; sql, args, err := query.toSQL()

patterns.name: sqlbuilder.ClickHouse for all SQL
patterns.description: Build SQL via github.com/huandu/go-sqlbuilder ClickHouse flavour. Never fmt.Sprintf WHERE values — use builder.Var()/Equal()/Where() to keep parameterisation safe and SQL assertions stable.

patterns.name: columnFactory for table-qualified columns
patterns.description: Use columnFactory(eventsTableName) to emit table-qualified column names (om_events.namespace) whenever CTEs or joins could introduce ambiguity.

patterns.name: toCountRowSQL companion for progress tracking
patterns.description: Query structs that may be tracked via progressmanager also implement toCountRowSQL() counting only by ORDER-BY columns for a cheap estimate. Connector checks params.ClientID != nil before calling withProgressContext.
patterns.example: if params.ClientID != nil { countSQL, countArgs := query.toCountRowSQL(); ctx, err = c.withProgressContext(ctx, namespace, *params.ClientID, countSQL, countArgs) }

patterns.name: ClickHouse code 60 mapped to domain errors
patterns.description: Map ClickHouse error string 'code: 60' (table/view not found) to models.NewNamespaceNotFoundError or meterpkg.NewMeterNotFoundError. Never let raw ClickHouse errors escape.

patterns.name: Coordinated customer filtering
patterns.description: Customer filtering needs two calls: selectCustomerIdColumn builds a WITH map CTE and adds customer_id to SELECT; customersWhere adds the IN (subjects) WHERE clause. Both take eventsTableName.
patterns.example: query = selectCustomerIdColumn(d.EventsTableName, *d.Customers, query); query = customersWhere(d.EventsTableName, *d.Customers, query)

patterns.name: SkipCreateTables=true in unit tests
patterns.description: New() calls createTable on startup; mock connections have no DDL Exec setup. Set Config.SkipCreateTables=true when constructing against a mock.
patterns.example: config := Config{..., SkipCreateTables: true}; connector, err := New(ctx, config)

key_file_guides.role: Public surface implementing streaming.Connector; owns Config.Validate(), createTable on startup, orchestrates all query structs. Method bodies are thin delegators.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.SkipCreateTables must be true in unit tests or New() calls createTable on a mock with no Exec setup.

key_file_guides.role: Most complex query struct: all aggregations (sum/avg/min/max/count/uniqExact/argMax), window tumble functions, group-by JSON extraction, stored-at filter, PREWHERE split, NullDecimal scanner, scanRows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Month window size needs explicit toDateTime(..., tz) cast to avoid Date-vs-DateTime scan mismatch. from() merges query.From with Meter.EventFrom taking the later.

key_file_guides.role: v1 event listing (queryEventsTable), count (queryCountEvents), batch insert (InsertEventsQuery), and table DDL (createEventsTable).
key_file_guides.watch_for: InsertEventsQuery.ToSQL is exported (capital T) unlike other structs. INSERT column order must match the table definition exactly.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor-paginated v2 listing using store_row_id (ULID) as tie-breaker; cursor conditions differ between ASC and DESC.
key_file_guides.watch_for: customersWhere in v2 passes full tableName (database.table) while v1 passes only eventsTableName — keep consistent with the helper expectation.

key_file_guides.role: Shared helpers: selectCustomerIdColumn (WITH map CTE), customersWhere, subjectWhere, columnFactory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetUsageAttribution().GetValues() may return customer key + subject keys; both are mapped in the WITH clause.

key_file_guides.role: CHTestSuite: skips integration tests unless TEST_CLICKHOUSE_DSN is set, creates a unique temp DB per test, drops it on success only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use t.Context() not context.Background(); cleanup intentionally skips DROP on failure to aid debugging.

key_file_guides.role: MockClickHouse and MockRows for unit tests without a real connection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MockClickHouse.Query must be set up before query methods are called — the Connector calls Query synchronously.

anti_patterns: Using fmt.Sprintf string interpolation for query values instead of sqlbuilder.Var()/builder parameters.
Calling c.config.ClickHouse.Query/Exec directly in a new method without a query-struct toSQL().
Returning raw ClickHouse errors instead of mapping code 60 to NamespaceNotFoundError / MeterNotFoundError.
Adding window-size logic to scanRows — scanRows only scans; toSQL handles window column selection.
Skipping toCountRowSQL on a new progress-trackable query struct — withProgressContext must run before the main query.

decisions.decision: Single shared events table across namespaces with namespace as leading ORDER BY column.
decisions.rationale: Avoids per-namespace table churn; partition pruning by toYYYYMM(time) plus the sort key gives adequate multi-tenant read performance.

decisions.decision: Query structs with toSQL() instead of inline SQL.
decisions.rationale: Enables unit tests to assert exact SQL without a live ClickHouse and keeps each query independently testable.
ClickHouse DateTime is second-precision; same-second events would order non-deterministically without a unique time-ordered tie-breaker.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new meter aggregation query that must be unit-testable

openmeter/streaming/retry.purpose: Decorator that wraps any streaming.Connector with automatic retry for transient ClickHouse errors (EOF, connection-acquire timeout, cluster-reshard code). BatchInsert and ValidateJSONPath are intentionally NOT retried to preserve idempotency.

patterns.name: Decorator / pass-through wrapper
patterns.description: Connector holds only downstreamConnector + retry config. Read methods wrap the downstream call in withRetry; mutation/lifecycle methods (BatchInsert, ValidateJSONPath, CreateNamespace, DeleteNamespace) delegate directly without retry.
patterns.example: func (c *Connector) QueryMeter(...) { return withRetry(ctx, c, func() ([]meter.MeterQueryRow, error) { return c.downstreamConnector.QueryMeter(ctx, namespace, m, params) }) }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() enforced by New()
patterns.description: New() runs config.Validate() and errors on failure. Tests bypassing validation construct the Connector struct directly.
patterns.example: c, err := New(Config{DownstreamConnector: ..., Logger: slog.Default(), RetryWaitDuration: 100*time.Millisecond, MaxTries: 3})

patterns.name: Selective retry via RetryIf predicate
patterns.description: Only io.ErrUnexpectedEOF, io.EOF, clickhouse.ErrAcquireConnTimeout, and chproto.ErrAllConnectionTriesFailed are retryable; all else fails immediately. New retryable errors go in the RetryIf closure.
patterns.example: retry.RetryIf(func(err error) bool { if errors.Is(err, io.ErrUnexpectedEOF) { return true }; ... })

patterns.name: CombineDelay(BackOffDelay, RandomDelay) with MaxDelay cap
patterns.description: avast/retry-go with backoff + random jitter to avoid thundering-herd reconnects; MaxDelay caps exponential growth.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: Config, Connector, New, per-method wrappers, and generic withRetry[T any] using avast/retry-go with backoff+jitter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BatchInsert is intentionally NOT wrapped — retrying inserts duplicates events. CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace/ValidateJSONPath also bypass retry intentionally.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for Config validation, retry behaviour, context cancellation stopping retries, and backoff producing increasing delays.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests construct Connector directly (not via New()) to skip config validation when isolating withRetry — intentional.

anti_patterns: Wrapping BatchInsert with withRetry — duplicate inserts break idempotency.
Adding application errors (e.g. MeterNotFoundError) to RetryIf — only transient infrastructure errors retry.
Relying on MaxDelay=0 — Validate() accepts it but retry-go treats 0 as no cap, allowing very long backoff.
Constructing Connector without New() in production — skips config validation.

decisions.decision: Backoff + random jitter (CombineDelay) with configurable MaxDelay.
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse rolling restarts cause transient connection errors; jittered backoff avoids thundering-herd reconnect storms across concurrent workers.
Retrying inserts would duplicate rows; the sink worker's three-phase flush handles retry at a higher level.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap a new read-only Connector method with retry

openmeter/streaming/testutils.purpose: In-memory MockStreamingConnector for tests needing a streaming.Connector without a real ClickHouse. Supports AddSimpleEvent/SetSimpleEvents (with WithStoredAt), AddRow for pre-canned exact results, and approximate aggregation mirroring ClickHouse tumble-window semantics.

patterns.name: AddSimpleEvent for usage-based setup
patterns.description: Register per-meter events with AddSimpleEvent(meterSlug, value, time, opts...). WithStoredAt(t) sets StoredAt distinct from Time to exercise stored-at cutoff logic. Events auto-sort by Time ASC after each add.

patterns.name: AddRow for exact pre-canned QueryMeter results
patterns.description: Use AddRow(meterSlug, meter.MeterQueryRow{...}) when approximation is insufficient. QueryMeter returns rows whose WindowStart/WindowEnd exactly match params.From/To. AddRow takes precedence over SimpleEvents for that slug.
patterns.example: conn.AddRow("meter-key", meter.MeterQueryRow{WindowStart: from, WindowEnd: to, Value: 42.0, GroupBy: map[string]*string{}})

patterns.name: Reset() between subtests
patterns.description: Call conn.Reset() to clear rows and events between cases sharing a connector. AddSimpleEvent appends; rows accumulate unless Reset() is called.

patterns.name: filterStoredAt uses Unix-second precision
patterns.description: The mock evaluates FilterTimeUnix via storedAt.Unix() seconds, matching ClickHouse DateTime precision. Sub-second StoredAt distinctions are ignored.
patterns.example: // Use full-second boundaries for StoredAt filters in tests

patterns.name: MeterNotFoundError for unregistered slugs
patterns.description: If a slug was never registered via AddSimpleEvent/AddRow, QueryMeter returns meter.NewMeterNotFoundError. Register an empty slice via SetSimpleEvents to register without events.

key_file_guides.role: Whole package: MockStreamingConnector, SimpleEvent, AddSimpleEvent, SetSimpleEvents, AddRow, QueryMeter aggregation, filterStoredAt.
key_file_guides.watch_for: aggregateEvents returns MeterNotFoundError for never-registered slugs — register the slug first.

key_file_guides.role: Tests for approximation accuracy: windowed vs non-windowed, LATEST, StoredAt filtering, empty-window suppression.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests import openmeter/testutils (not this package) for GetRFC3339Time — beware import cycles when adding helpers.

anti_patterns: Using this mock for tests needing real ClickHouse SQL behaviour (PREWHERE, decimal edge cases) — use CHTestSuite with TEST_CLICKHOUSE_DSN.
Registering events for a slug after QueryMeter on it within the same test without Reset() — AddSimpleEvent appends.
Relying on sub-second StoredAt precision — FilterTimeUnix truncates to Unix seconds.
Calling NewMockStreamingConnector without passing testing.TB — t is required for helper registration.

decisions.decision: Approximate aggregation rather than full SQL semantics.
decisions.rationale: Full SQL emulation is fragile; the approximation suffices for service-layer tests while real ClickHouse via CHTestSuite covers exact SQL behaviour.

decisions.decision: AddRow takes precedence over SimpleEvents for the same slug.
decisions.rationale: Lets tests supply exact results for complex group-by/multi-window scenarios without reimplementing aggregation.

code_examples.scenario: Test a service querying meter usage with a stored-at cutoff
code_examples.code:
conn := testutils.NewMockStreamingConnector(t)
conn.AddSimpleEvent("api-calls", 5, now, testutils.WithStoredAt(now.Add(-time.Hour)))
conn.AddSimpleEvent("api-calls", 3, now, testutils.WithStoredAt(now.Add(time.Hour)))
rows, err := conn.QueryMeter(ctx, ns, meter, streaming.QueryParams{From: &from, To: &to, FilterStoredAt: &filter.FilterTimeUnix{FilterTime: filter.FilterTime{Lt: &now}}})
// rows[0].Value == 5
